summary: Discovery notes are written to the declared output path. I re-explored the repo for iteration 2, verifying all iteration-1 findings still hold and adding a new "Facts sharpening the iteration-2 critic feedback" section with concrete code evidence for each feedback item: F1 (only `open_questions` is currently rendered in the paused-style view via `DefineHelper#define_open_questions`; `discovery_notes` and other artifacts have no rendering path at all), F2 (the DAG can only advance while `Phase#running?`, and even the existing "start the whole phase" precedent is inherently multi-tick via async merge + 10s ticks, not a single atomic restart), F3 (failure/transient/merge/stuck paths all surface only as a badge, with every existing re-run action being fire-and-forget with no success/failure follow-through), and F4 (`Phases::Approve::APPROVABLE_STATUSES` is a hard-coded `%w[consensus awaiting_human]` guard, so "Done" from a new distinct `paused` status would be rejected unless extended). Also documented a previously-unmentioned existing primitive, `StepRuns::Queue` (the step card's "Re-run" link), which is directly relevant to how single-step menu actions might be modeled. The open-questions list was revised to incorporate these facts rather than restate them as unknowns.
